Hormones in Balance

=E2=80=8A

4 tips for PMS and menopause

Your hormones influence your whole body =E2=80=93 from energy and mood to s=
leep, skin, and weight. When hormones fluctuate, such as during PMS or meno=
pause, the effects can be felt both physically and emotionally.

With the right nutrition and dietary supplements, you can support your body=
 through these phases in a natural way. Small, sustainable changes can make=
 a big difference to your well-being over time.

=E2=80=8A

=E2=80=8A

Did you know that nutrient deficiencies can intensify hormonal symptoms?

[Discover our range for women=E2=80=99s health =E2=86=92](https://www.great=
life.eu/health-goal/womens-health)

=E2=80=8A

[Greatlife](https://www.greatlife.eu/health-goal/womens-health)

=E2=80=8A

Strengthen Your Hormonal Balance

=E2=80=8A

4 simple tips for PMS and menopause

=E2=80=8A

1. Support the body with the right vitamins and minerals
The hormonal system depends on proper nutrition to function optimally. [B v=
itamins](https://www.greatlife.eu/supplements/vitamins/vitamin-b) contribut=
e to normal hormonal balance and reduced fatigue. [Magnesium](https://www.g=
reatlife.eu/magnesium-greatlife) may help with irritability and tension. Du=
ring menopause, [vitamin D](https://www.greatlife.eu/vitamin-d3-2000-greatl=
ife), [zinc](https://www.greatlife.eu/zinc-picolinate-greatlife), and [sele=
nium](https://www.greatlife.eu/selenium-greatlife) are also important for b=
oth hormones and the thyroid.

[Supplements for hormonal balance =E2=86=92](https://www.greatlife.eu/healt=
h-goal/womens-health/menopause)

2. Take care of your thyroid
The thyroid is central to the body=E2=80=99s metabolism and energy levels. =
It is affected by nutritional status and requires [iodine](https://www.grea=
tlife.eu/liquid-iodine), [selenium](https://www.greatlife.eu/selenium-great=
life), and [zinc](https://www.greatlife.eu/zinc-picolinate-greatlife) to fu=
nction normally. Thyroid imbalance can cause symptoms such as fatigue, sens=
itivity to cold, weight changes, and mood swings.

[Supplements for hormones and thyroid =E2=86=92](https://www.greatlife.eu/h=
ealth-goal/hormones-thyroid)

3. Reduce inflammation and blood sugar fluctuations
Large swings in blood sugar can worsen PMS symptoms such as mood changes an=
d sugar cravings. A balanced diet with protein, healthy fats, and fiber hel=
ps create more stable energy levels. [Omega-3 fatty](https://www.greatlife.=
eu/wild-alaskan-sockeye-salmon) acids can help support the body=E2=80=99s n=
atural balance and reduce inflammatory processes.

[Buy omega-3 =E2=86=92](https://www.greatlife.eu/wild-alaskan-sockeye-salmo=
n)

4. Prioritize sleep and recovery
Sleep is essential for hormonal recovery. During the night, hormones such a=
s the stress hormone cortisol and sex hormones like progesterone and estrog=
en are regulated. Sleep disturbances are common during menopause.

[Supplements for better sleep =E2=86=92](https://www.greatlife.eu/health-go=
al/sleep-mental-health/sleep)
